_work.title: Seals of the 72 Spirits (Ars Goetia)
_work.creator: various
_work.rights: Public domain / CC0 / one CC BY-SA (per item). Via Wikimedia Commons.
_work.blurb: The individual seals of the seventy-two demons of the Ars Goetia, the first book of the Lesser Key of Solomon — each spirit's proper signature, by which the conjurer named, summoned and bound it, with its rank, office and legions per the Mathers/Crowley edition of 1904.

entries.title: The Seal of Vassago (Prince of the Goetia)
entries.creator: after the Lemegeton; ed. S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley
entries.date: MS tradition 17th c.; this rendering after the 1904 edition
entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Vassago, the prince ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Vassago is a [[motif:prince|Prince]] commanding 26 legions. He of good nature; declares things past and to come and discovers things hidden or lost. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia: The Lesser Key of Solomon the King* (1904).

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Samigina, the marquis ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Samigina is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 30 legions. He also called Gamigin; teaches the liberal sciences and gives account of the souls of the dead who died in sin. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

The seal (*sigillum*) of Marbas, the president ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Marbas is a [[motif:president|President]] commanding 36 legions. He answers truly of hidden or secret things, causes and heals diseases, and transforms men into other shapes. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Valefor, the duke ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Valefor is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 10 legions. He a good familiar, but tempts those he is familiar with to steal; appears as a lion with an ass's head. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Barbatos is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 30 legions. entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Paimon, the king ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Paimon is a [[motif:king|King]] commanding 200 legions. He teaches all arts, sciences and secrets; gives good familiars and binds men to the magician's will; appears crowned, riding a dromedary. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

The seal (*sigillum*) of Botis, the president and earl ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Botis is a [[motif:president|President and Earl]] commanding 60 legions. He tells of things past and to come and reconciles friends and foes; appears first as a viper, then as a horned human. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Sallos is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 30 legions. ## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Purson is a [[motif:king|King]] commanding 22 legions. In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Marax is a [[motif:earl|Earl and President]] commanding 30 legions. An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Ipos is a [[motif:earl|Earl and Prince]] commanding 36 legions. He knows things past and to come; makes men witty and bold; appears as an angel with a lion's head, goose's feet and a hare's tail. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Aim is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 26 legions. He also Aym or Haborym; sets cities and great places afire and makes men witty; appears three-headed, riding a viper, bearing a firebrand. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Naberius, the marquis ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Naberius is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 19 legions. He also Cerberus; makes men cunning in arts and sciences, especially rhetoric, and restores lost dignities and honours. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

The seal (*sigillum*) of Glasya-Labolas, the president ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Glasya-Labolas is a [[motif:president|President]] commanding 36 legions. He author of bloodshed and manslaughter; teaches all arts and sciences in an instant and gives the gift of invisibility. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Ronove is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis and Earl]] commanding 19 legions. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. ## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Berith is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 26 legions. He also Beale or Bolfry; tells of things past, present and to come, turns metals into gold, and gives dignities; speaks with a clear, subtle voice. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Forneus is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 29 legions. He teaches rhetoric and languages, gives men a good name, and procures the love of friends and foes; appears as a sea-monster. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Gaap, the prince and president ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Gaap is a [[motif:prince|Prince and President]] commanding 66 legions. He teaches philosophy and the liberal sciences, causes love or hatred, makes men insensible, and transports men swiftly between kingdoms. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

The seal (*sigillum*) of Phenex, the marquis ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Phenex is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 20 legions. He a marvellous poet; speaks sweetly like a child and sings; teaches the sciences and hopes to return to the seventh throne after 1,200 years. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Malphas is a [[motif:president|President]] commanding 40 legions. He builds houses and high towers, brings the artificers' tools, gives good familiars, and receives sacrifices kindly though he deceives. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

entries.title: The Seal of Focalor (Duke of the Goetia)
entries.creator: after the Lemegeton; ed. S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley
entries.date: MS tradition 17th c.; this rendering after the 1904 edition
entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Focalor, the duke ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Focalor is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 30 legions (or 3). He has power over wind and sea; drowns men and overturns warships; appears as a man with a griffin's wings. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia: The Lesser Key of Solomon the King* (1904).

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Vepar is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 29 legions. He also Separ; guides the waters and ships laden with arms; can cause the seas to rough and bring death by putrefying wounds. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Sabnock, the marquis ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Sabnock is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 50 legions. He builds high towers, castles and cities, furnishes them with arms, and afflicts men with wounds and putrefying sores; rides a pale horse, lion-headed. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Vine, the king and earl ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Vine is a [[motif:king|King and Earl]] commanding 36 legions. He discovers hidden things, witches and witchcraft, tells of things past, present and to come, and builds towers and breaks down walls; appears lion-headed on a black horse. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Vual is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 37 legions. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Haagenti is a [[motif:president|President]] commanding 33 legions. He makes men wise, turns all metals into gold, and changes wine into water and water into wine; appears as a great bull with griffin's wings. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Crocell is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 48 legions. He also Procell; teaches geometry and the liberal sciences, warms waters and discovers baths, and speaks mystically of hidden things. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Furcas is a [[motif:knight|Knight]] commanding 20 legions. He teaches philosophy, astronomy, rhetoric, logic, chiromancy and pyromancy; appears as a cruel old man riding a pale horse, bearing a sharp weapon. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Orobas is a [[motif:prince|Prince]] commanding 20 legions. He faithful and not deceitful; gives true answers of things past, present and to come, of divinity and the creation, and bestows dignities and favour. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Gremory is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 26 legions. An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Valac is a [[motif:president|President]] commanding 30 legions. He gives true answers of hidden treasures and reveals where serpents may be found, delivering them harmless; appears as a child with angel's wings on a two-headed dragon. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Belial is a [[motif:king|King]] commanding 80 legions. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Seere is a [[motif:prince|Prince]] commanding 26 legions. He of good nature; brings things to pass suddenly, carries goods anywhere, and discovers thefts and hidden treasures; rides a winged horse. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Andromalius, the earl ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Andromalius is a [[motif:earl|Earl]] commanding 36 legions. He returns stolen goods, discovers thieves and hidden treasures, punishes wickedness and dishonest dealing; appears holding a great serpent. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia: The Lesser Key of Solomon the King* (1904).

entries.title: The Seal of Astaroth (Duke of the Goetia)

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Astaroth, the duke ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Astaroth is a [[motif:duke|Duke]] commanding 40 legions. He gives true answers of things past, present and to come and of the secret of the Fall; teaches the liberal sciences; appears as a foul angel on an infernal dragon. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia: The Lesser Key of Solomon the King* (1904).

entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Murmur, the duke and earl ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Murmur is a [[motif:duke|Duke and Earl]] commanding 30 legions. He teaches philosophy and constrains the souls of the dead to appear and answer questions; appears as a warrior on a griffin, with a ducal crown. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

entries.title: The Seal of Andras (Marquis of the Goetia)

entries.date: MS tradition 17th c.; this rendering after the 1904 edition
entries.summary:
The seal (*sigillum*) of Andras, the marquis ranked among the seventy-two spirits of the *Ars Goetia*.

## Iconography
An abstract line-glyph composed of crosses, loops, arcs and points — the character the conjurer engraves or draws to summon and bind the spirit, worn as a *Lamen* before the breast.

## Significance
In the *Ars Goetia*, the first book of the *Lesser Key of Solomon* (*Lemegeton*), Andras is a [[motif:marquis|Marquis]] commanding 30 legions. He sows discord; comes to slay the master and his servants if not carefully governed; appears with an angel's body and a raven's head, riding a black wolf. The seal is his proper signature; with the Brass Vessel, the Magic Circle and Triangle, and the conjurations of S. L. MacGregor Mathers and Aleister Crowley's edition (1904), it compels his appearance.

## For artists & game designers
A ready-made summoning glyph for a demon-binding system: each of the 72 spirits has a distinct seal, rank, and bargainable office.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia: The Lesser Key of Solomon the King* (1904).

entries.title: The 72 Demons of the Ars Goetia (composite plate of seals)
entries.creator: after the Lemegeton (Lesser Key of Solomon)
entries.date: compiled from the 1904 edition tradition
entries.summary:
A composite plate arraying the seals of all seventy-two demons of the *Ars Goetia* in a single grid.

## Iconography
Seventy-two distinct line-sigils, each labelled with its spirit's name, ranked from Bael through Andromalius — the full roster of the *Lesser Key of Solomon*'s first book.

## Significance
The *Ars Goetia* catalogues 72 spirits, each with a name, a rank ([[motif:king|king]], [[motif:duke|duke]], prince, marquis, president, earl or knight), an office, a number of legions, and a proper seal used to conjure and bind it. The system descends from Johann Weyer's *Pseudomonarchia Daemonum* (1577) and was codified in the *Lemegeton*, popularised by Mathers and Crowley (1904).

## For artists & game designers
A single reference sheet for a full demon-summoning roster — 72 ranked entities each keyed to a unique glyph.

citations.text: S. L. MacGregor Mathers & Aleister Crowley (eds.), *The Goetia* (1904).
